records.ety: [OE. honeste, oneste, honor, OF. honest, onest (cf. F. honntet), L. honestas. See Honest, a.]

defs: Honor; honorableness; dignity; propriety; suitableness; decency.
quotes: She derives her honesty and achieves her goodness.

defs: The quality or state of being honest; probity; fairness and straightforwardness of conduct, speech, etc.; integrity; sincerity; truthfulness; freedom from fraud or guile.
quotes: That we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and honesty.
To lay . . . siege to the honesty of this Ford's wife.

defs: Satin flower; the name of two cruciferous herbs having large flat pods, the round shining partitions of which are more beautiful than the blossom; -- called also lunary and moonwort. Lunaria biennis is common honesty; Lunaria rediva is perennial honesty.
